summary: The main claim is: None of the presidents fully represent my (TurnTrout's) views.
Garrett Baker helped produce the video after the audio was complete.

top_comment.author: Viliam
top_comment.text: This is the future of education. I don't think I would have otherwise spent 22 minutes listening to a discussion of pros and cons of various research agendas.
top_comment.claim: The comment's point: I don't think I would have otherwise spent 22 minutes listening to a discussion of pros and cons of various research agendas.

summary: The main claim is: Please do not interpret this as a helpful overview of the problem, the list of dynamics here is extremely far from exhaustive, and this inadequacy should be kept in mind when forming functioning world models.
The main claim is: Each of these personality traits is heritable, so these values are also heritable.
Why does the heritability of human values matter for AI alignment?
The main claim is: This would of course also only occur if the development of AI is slow enough for the Internet to "fill up" with AI-generated content.
Maybe I'm underestimating the amount of content on the internet or overestimating the use of AI.
The main claim is: Therefore, we can equate instrumental convergence from the AI alignment discourse with agents minimising their expected free energy in the Active Inference framework.
I don't understand whether AGI agents must unavoidably be Active Inference agents, and, therefore, exhibit instrumental convergence.
The main claim is: A slightly more technical definition is that an agent is corrigible if it is easy for other agents to optimize over it.
You can't optimize its output by optimizing its input.
